input_text: I clicked the link, entered my bank details and OTP, and then Rs 25000 was debited from my account through UPI. Money is gone.

result.summary: Immediately call 1930 or visit cybercrime.gov.in to report the transaction and contact your bank to freeze your account.
result.conversational_reply: Immediately call 1930 or visit cybercrime.gov.in to report the transaction and contact your bank to freeze your account.

reporting_recommendation.reason: Potential compromise detected (possible money loss, possible malicious link interaction). Report now and begin immediate protective actions.

presentation_sections.headline: Analysis complete

response_sections.overview: Immediately call 1930 or visit cybercrime.gov.in to report the transaction and contact your bank to freeze your account.
next_steps: Call the National Cyber Crime Helpline to report the transaction and initiate a 'Lien' on the beneficiary account.
Report the UPI transaction and request a block on the UPI ID/VPA used for the debit.
Change NetBanking passwords and UPI PINs immediately.
Immediately call 1930 or visit cybercrime.gov.in to report the transaction and contact your bank to freeze your account.

result.presentation_markdown:
## What this looks like
Immediately call 1930 or visit cybercrime.gov.in to report the transaction and contact your bank to freeze your account.

## Why I'm saying that
- Unauthorized debit after clicking link
- Phishing for bank details
- OTP interception
- Immediate financial loss

## What to do now
1. Call the National Cyber Crime Helpline to report the transaction and initiate a 'Lien' on the beneficiary account.
2. Report the UPI transaction and request a block on the UPI ID/VPA used for the debit.
3. Change NetBanking passwords and UPI PINs immediately.
4. Immediately call 1930 or visit cybercrime.gov.in to report the transaction and contact your bank to freeze your account.

## Reporting
Potential compromise detected (possible money loss, possible malicious link interaction). Report now and begin immediate protective actions.
action_steps: Call the National Cyber Crime Helpline to report the transaction and initiate a 'Lien' on the beneficiary account.

complaint_draft.title: Cyber Fraud Complaint Draft
complaint_draft.body: The victim received a malicious link and was induced to enter bank credentials and an OTP. Subsequently, an unauthorized UPI transaction of Rs. 25,000 was debited from the victim's account. This is a clear case of phishing and financial fraud.

detection.victim_advice: Immediately call 1930 or visit cybercrime.gov.in to report the transaction and contact your bank to freeze your account.
detection.summary: The victim fell prey to a phishing attack where sensitive banking credentials and an OTP were harvested via a malicious link. This resulted in an unauthorized UPI debit of Rs 25,000 from the victim's account.

priority_actions.action: Call the National Cyber Crime Helpline to report the transaction and initiate a 'Lien' on the beneficiary account.
priority_actions.why_critical: The CFCFRMS system allows for real-time freezing of funds in the fraudster's bank account before they are withdrawn.

priority_actions.action: Report the UPI transaction and request a block on the UPI ID/VPA used for the debit.
priority_actions.contact_details: Bank's official mobile app or toll-free number
priority_actions.why_critical: Prevents further unauthorized debits and initiates the internal bank dispute process.

priority_actions.action: Change NetBanking passwords and UPI PINs immediately.
priority_actions.why_critical: The attacker has the victim's bank details and OTP access; the account remains vulnerable until credentials are changed.

section_ipc: Section 66D IT Act (Cheating by personation using computer resource)
Section 420 IPC (Cheating and dishonestly inducing delivery of property)
fir_template.case_summary: The victim received a malicious link and was induced to enter bank credentials and an OTP. Subsequently, an unauthorized UPI transaction of Rs. 25,000 was debited from the victim's account. This is a clear case of phishing and financial fraud.
evidence_to_preserve: Bank Account Statement showing the debit
Screenshot of the phishing webpage if still accessible
